task: Fix macOS SSL certificate verification error in Discord webhook client by integrating certifi library
discussion_summary: User reported SSL certificate verification failure when Discord notifications attempted to send. Root cause identified as macOS Python not using system certificates by default. Solution: use certifi library to provide proper SSL context for both `send_message` and `send_file` methods in discord.py. Changes staged but final commit message was incomplete in transcript.

accomplishments.what: Added certifi SSL context to discord.py send_message method
Added certifi SSL context to discord.py send_file method

in_progress.what: SSL context integration for webhook requests — both send_message and send_file methods updated to use certifi.where() for certificate verification

next_actions.action: Complete the git commit with full message: git commit -m 'fix: use certifi for macOS SSL certificate verification in Discord client'
next_actions.why: Commit was started but message was truncated in transcript; changes are staged and ready

next_actions.action: Have user run `pip install -r requirements.txt` to install certifi in their environment
next_actions.why: New dependency must be installed before Discord notifications will work

next_actions.action: Test Discord notification flow end-to-end to verify SSL error is resolved
next_actions.why: Confirm the fix resolves the [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] error in production

next_actions.action: Review implementation_state.md and roadmap.md for uncommitted changes and decide whether to commit or discard
next_actions.why: These files were modified during session but are state tracking files; need explicit decision on whether changes should persist

decisions.decision: Use certifi library for SSL certificate verification instead of disabling SSL verification or using system certificates directly
decisions.reason: certifi is the standard Python approach for cross-platform certificate handling; safer than disabling verification, more reliable than system certs on macOS

failed_approaches.approach: Checking if return value being a tuple breaks callers that use it as a bool
failed_approaches.reason: Determined to be a non-issue — Python doesn't care about unused return values, and the two callers that check the return value were already updated in previous edits

relational_files.why: Calls discord.py send methods; return value handling was verified to be compatible with tuple return
CLI entry point that triggers notifications; error messages now show actual HTTP/SSL errors instead of generic 'Discord send failed'

blockers: Git commit message was incomplete in transcript — needs to be finished with proper message text
